publications.title: Structural basis of biased T cell receptor recognition of an immunodominant HLA-A2 epitope of the SARS-CoV-2 spike protein.
publications.authors: Chaurasia P, Nguyen THO, Rowntree LC, Juno JA, Wheatley AK, Kent SJ, Kedzierska K, Rossjohn J, Petersen J.
publications.executive_summary: CD8+ T cells recognize the immunodominant S269-277 peptide from SARS-CoV-2 spike protein presented by HLA-A*02:01, with a striking bias toward TCRs expressing the TRAV12 gene segment. Crystal and cryo-EM structures of TRAV12+ TCRs bound to the HLA-A2/S269-277 complex reveal extensive contacts across the full length of the peptide, explaining both the dominance of this TCR segment and the sensitivity of these T cells to sequence variation. Despite this immunodominance during SARS-CoV-2 infection, TRAV12+ TCRs show poor cross-reactivity with analogous peptides from viral variants and related coronaviruses, as demonstrated by surface plasmon resonance and tetramer binding studies. This structural framework elucidates how a highly focused TCR repertoire can achieve dominant immunity against a single epitope while remaining poorly equipped to recognize antigenically similar variants, with implications for vaccine design and understanding the robustness of CD8+ T cell responses to evolving SARS-CoV-2 strains.
